passedAssertions.title: Authenticated requests receive a Bedrock or Z.AI response through the gateway
passedAssertions.evidence: End-to-end flow verified: login → proxy → runtime → gateway → Z.AI → UI response. Network analysis shows 100% traffic on draft.choir-ip.com, zero direct provider calls. Real LLM responses confirmed (sample: 'Quantum computing is a type of computing that uses the principles of quantum mechanics...'). Tasks complete in ~2.5s.

failedAssertions.title: Concurrent first requests for one user collapse onto one VM assignment
failedAssertions.reason: VM boot failure prevents end-to-end validation. VMs fail with 'truncate data.img: no such file or directory'. Concurrent collapsing mechanism exists in code (pendingWaiters) and shows single VM creation for some users, but race condition evidence exists (two VMs created for same user at 00:47:59).

details.vmBootError: truncate data image /var/lib/go-choir/vm-state/.../data.img: no such file or directory
details.rootCause: VM state directory not created before data.img truncation attempt
details.raceCondition: Logs show user a90fdeda-7a69-444a-adbd-bf61a827a906 had TWO VMs created at 00:47:59 (vm-3161... and vm-0dd3a...)
details.fallbackBehavior: System falls back to host-process sandbox (sandbox-m1) when VM boot fails

appliedUpdates.description: Updated VAL-GATEWAY-001 status to passed - end-to-end provider flow verified working after Node B redeployment
Updated VAL-VM-004 with new findings: VM boot failure persists (data.img creation), race condition evidence documented

deploymentStatus.commitMessage: Fix sandbox-vm.nix: use upstream microvm.nix API (storeOnDisk, storeDiskType)

fixedFromRound4: VAL-GATEWAY-001 now passes - end-to-end provider flow works correctly
Guest images rebuilt with upstream microvm.nix configuration
Real LLM responses confirmed through gateway → Z.AI path
remainingBlocker: VM boot still fails - new symptom: data.img directory creation race
VAL-VM-004 cannot be fully validated until VMs boot successfully
Concurrent request collapsing code exists but race conditions observed
verifiedWorking: All gateway HTTP assertions (VAL-GATEWAY-002 through 008 from prior rounds)

rootCauseAnalysis.gatewaySuccess: Gateway → provider integration works correctly. Z.AI provider responds with real LLM output. Host-process fallback mode allows end-to-end validation even when VMs fail.

vmBootIssue.currentSymptom: Firecracker boot failed: create data image ... truncate data.img: no such file or directory
vmBootIssue.analysis: Guest image rebuild with microvm.nix changed error manifestation. Now failing at data image creation step rather than init execution. Suggests directory creation ordering issue in vmmanager.BootVM.
vmBootIssue.codeLocation: Likely in internal/vm/vmmanager.go BootVM() - data.img truncation happens before directory creation or in wrong order

raceCondition.evidence: User a90fdeda-7a69-444a-adbd-bf61a827a906 had TWO VMs created at same timestamp (00:47:59) in logs
raceCondition.mechanism: pendingWaiters exists but race window between mutex unlock and channel registration allows duplicate VM creation
raceCondition.impact: Without working VMs, cannot definitively prove concurrent collapsing works end-to-end

notes: Round 5 re-run after Node B redeployment with microvm.nix guest image fixes
VAL-GATEWAY-001 PASSED - major milestone: real provider-backed responses confirmed
VAL-VM-004 still blocked by VM boot failure - data.img creation race needs fix
1 of 2 retested assertions now passing (50% improvement)
Gateway functionality is production-ready even with VM fallback mode
VM concurrent collapsing code exists but needs VM health to validate end-to-end
recommendations: Fix VM state directory creation order in vmmanager.BootVM() - ensure mkdir before data.img truncation
Add explicit directory creation: os.MkdirAll(vmStateDir, 0750) before data image operations
Investigate race condition window in pendingWaiters - consider sync.Cond or stricter mutex scope
Add logging for concurrent request detection to help debug collapsing behavior
After VM boot fix, re-run VAL-VM-004 to confirm concurrent collapsing works end-to-end
